request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will TikTok be banned in the United States by April 30, 2025?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if, on or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is unavailable in the United States
via the Apple App Store or the Google Play Store, OR a federal directive (PAFACA
enforcement order from the Department of Commerce / Department of Justice) takes
effect requiring a divestiture or shutdown. Resolves NO otherwise.

Conditions:
  A: TikTok absent in US Apple App Store — On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Apple App Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).
  B: TikTok absent in US Google Play — On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Google Play Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).
  C: Federal PAFACA order takes effect — On or before 2025-04-30, a DOC or DOJ PAFACA enforcement order takes effect requiring TikTok/ByteDance to divest or shut down US operations.
Settlement entities: ['Federal Communications Commission', 'Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States', 'Department of Commerce', 'ByteDance', 'TikTok']

Record title: February
Record date: 2025-02-06
Record regulator: Alaska Board of Nursing
Record topic: Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
Record entities: ['Alaska Board of Nursing']

response.one_line_why: Alaska Board of Nursing item is unrelated to TikTok availability or any federal PAFACA enforcement; it doesn't change ban odds.
